summary:
Despite its simplicity and speed, standard technique faces challenges due to inaccurate shaping that may lead to inadequate removal of accessories canals; thus limiting widespread use for fear of compromised results after filling procedures.
To further clarify why this method isn't widely adopted despite ease-of-use, consider how it fails at fully preparing certain areas within root canals which could result in treatment failure if left unaddressed during endodontic therapy sessions involving obturation steps afterward. Agent1's reasoning focuses primarily on two critical factors hindering frequent usage – accuracy concerns regarding proper filing shapes and difficulties addressing hidden or small side passages ('accessory' canals) crucially needed before final sealant application. These shortcomings contribute significantly towards higher risk profiles when opting solely over modern alternatives designed specifically targeting these deficiencies through improved design features To further clarify why this method isn't widely adopted despite ease-of-use, consider how it fails at fully preparing certain areas within root canals which could result in treatment failure if left unaddressed during endodontic therapy sessions involving obturation steps afterward. Agent1's reasoning focuses primarily on two critical factors hindering frequent usage – accuracy concerns regarding proper filing shapes and difficulties addressing hidden or small side passages ('accessory' canals) crucially needed before final sealant application.
